issues.description: FR-5b data inconsistency: handle_cancel backfill uses literal "Cancelled" but LoopState::Cancelled.to_string() returns "CANCELLED" (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE per the Display impl). The tick() backfill at line 122 correctly uses `&new_state.to_string()`. This creates inconsistent `loop_final_state` values in the judge_decisions table — cancelled loops will store "Cancelled" while all other terminal states store their SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE variant (e.g., "CONVERGED", "FAILED"). This corrupts the Stage 2 training dataset since the same logical state has two different string representations.
issues.suggestion: Replace `"Cancelled"` with `&LoopState::Cancelled.to_string()` or the literal `"CANCELLED"` to match the Display implementation.

issues.description: Review path Continue-at-max-rounds discards judge reasoning in the failure message. The failure_reason is set to the generic `"Max implement rounds ({}) exceeded"` without including `judge_output.reasoning`. In contrast, the equivalent harden path (line 867) correctly includes it: `"Max harden rounds ({}) exceeded. Judge wanted to continue: {}"`. The judge's analysis of why it wanted to continue is valuable diagnostic information for the engineer, especially at max_rounds.
issues.suggestion: Change the failure_reason to include judge reasoning: `format!("Max implement rounds ({}) exceeded. Judge wanted to continue: {}", record.max_rounds, judge_output.reasoning)`

issues.description: handle_cancel returns early (line 95) before the tick() backfill block (line 118), so the backfill was added directly inside handle_cancel. However, the tick() backfill block at line 118 will ALSO run for non-cancel terminal transitions that happen in the same tick. The two backfill implementations use different state string formatting (literal vs .to_string()). While only one path runs per terminal transition, the code duplication creates a maintenance risk — future changes to the backfill logic must be updated in two places.
issues.suggestion: Restructure handle_cancel to not return early, instead assigning its result to new_state so the single backfill block at line 118 handles all terminal transitions uniformly. This eliminates the duplicated backfill code and the string format inconsistency.

issues.description: FR-1b spec deviation: determine_trigger returns None for rounds < 3 without recurring findings, skipping the judge entirely. FR-1b states the judge is invoked when `verdict.clean == false` (any round). While this is a reasonable budget optimization (preserving calls for later ambiguous decisions), it means the judge cannot override-accept a round-1 or round-2 verdict with only trivial low-severity findings — exactly Problem 1 from the spec. A round-2 verdict with five low-severity nits and no recurring findings will always loop, defeating the judge's purpose.
issues.suggestion: Consider skipping only round 1 (not round 2), or add an additional condition that checks if all current issues are low-severity before skipping. Alternatively, document this as an intentional deviation from FR-1b.

issues.description: The round_summaries BTreeMap is keyed by RoundRecord.round, but multiple RoundRecords can share the same round number (e.g., implement round 3, test round 3, review round 3). The `or_insert_with` creates one RoundSummary per unique round number, merging stages. However, judge decisions are also keyed by `d.round` which is `LoopRecord.round` at invocation time. If LoopRecord.round is incremented between the review verdict parse and the judge invocation (e.g., in the Continue path at line 1158: `record.round += 1`), the judge decision's round won't match any round_summary entry. The decision would only appear in the flat array, not nested inline.
issues.suggestion: Verify that the judge decision is always persisted with the round number BEFORE any increment, or adjust the nesting lookup to check d.round and d.round-1.

issues.description: JudgeDecision has both serde Deserialize (with snake_case rename) and a manual parse_decision method. While the test_judge_decision_serde_parse_roundtrip test verifies they stay in sync, the parse_decision method is not used anywhere in the implementation — all deserialization goes through serde. This is dead code.
issues.suggestion: Remove parse_decision if unused, or use it in parse_judge_response as a fallback parser.

issues.description: OrchestratorJudge::new is async solely for load_judge_prompt().await which does a single tokio::fs::read_to_string. While fine for production (runs once at startup), it forces all test constructors to be async. The prompt could be loaded lazily or passed as a parameter to simplify testing.
issues.suggestion: Accept the prompt template as a parameter in the constructor, with a separate async factory method for production use. This would simplify test setup.
